question: how does logrus add hooks to log entries?

notes.fact: Hook interface = Levels() []Level + Fire(*Entry) error. Both required for any hook implementation.
LevelHooks is map[Level][]Hook — the registry data structure indexes hooks by the levels they care about.
LevelHooks.Add(hook): iterates hook.Levels() and appends hook to the slice for each level. Same hook can land in multiple level buckets.

LevelHooks.Fire(level, entry): walks hooks[level] and calls Fire on each; returns first error and stops. Note this is the LevelHooks.Fire on the registry — the actual log path uses entry.fireHooks (see entry.go) which logs errors to stderr instead of returning them.
Logger struct field: Hooks LevelHooks — the per-logger hook registry, exported and directly mutatable (documented usage).
New() initializes Hooks via make(LevelHooks). Default loggers therefore start with an empty but non-nil registry.
Logger.AddHook(hook): acquires logger.mu, then delegates to logger.Hooks.Add(hook). Mutex protects against concurrent AddHook + log calls.

Logger.hooksForLevel(level): under the lock, copies the []Hook slice for that level and returns it so callers can iterate without holding the mutex (avoids deadlocks if a hook calls back into the logger).
Logger.ReplaceHooks: atomically swaps the entire LevelHooks map under the mutex, returning the previous one.
Entry struct holds a *Logger pointer, plus Data/Time/Level/Caller/Message/Buffer/Context. The Entry is what hooks receive and may mutate before formatting/output.

Entry.log(level, msg) is the central log path: dups the entry, sets Time/Level/Message, optionally fills Caller, then calls logger.hooksForLevel(level) and newEntry.fireHooks(...) BEFORE formatting and writing output.

Hooks fire BEFORE the formatter runs. They receive a pointer to the Entry and may mutate fields (e.g. Data) — those mutations are visible to the formatter on the same call. The hook list is snapshotted before fireHooks, so AddHook calls during firing don't affect this event.

Entry.fireHooks: iterates the snapshotted []Hook, calls hook.Fire(entry) on each. On any error, prints 'Failed to fire hook:' to stderr and returns early — remaining hooks are skipped. The error is NOT returned to the caller.
Package-level std = New() — the implicit global Logger used by top-level helpers.
Top-level logrus.AddHook(hook) is just std.AddHook(hook). Registers hooks on the global default logger.
Reference Hook implementation: stores Entries with a sync.RWMutex. Compile-time assertion `var _ logrus.Hook = (*Hook)(nil)` shows the canonical interface-conformance idiom.
Implements Fire (appends a copy of the entry under lock) and Levels (returns logrus.AllLevels — fires for every level).
